resolvedQuestions: Which packet documentation states still claim Complete while evidence, checklist, metadata, or continuity contradicts them?
Which review/research lineage artifacts are stale, abandoned, unreconciled, or misleading after later fixes?
Which runtime scripts or workflow YAMLs contain bugs or brittle contracts affecting fan-out, salvage, merge, convergence, or timeout behavior?
Which phase packets need stronger evidence, tests, ADR/checklist records, or documentation-map updates before completion can be trusted?
What safety, observability, and loop-system hardening upgrades would most reduce future drift?

keyFindings.title: Complete status conflicts with Draft child maps and stale continuity
Review lineages remain CONDITIONAL/active after 007 fan-out-hardening claims fixes shipped
Workflow YAML still contains ephemeral F-010-B5 markers
Deep-research fan-out does not pass resolved convergence threshold into fanout-run
Per-lineage timeout hard-caps at 4h with no override, undercutting 30+ high-effort iterations
Root and remediation graph metadata omit real implementation surfaces
ADR/checklist governance artifacts are missing for Complete Level 2/3 ADR-style phases
Validator and workflow gaps allow recurrence

ruledOutDirections: Treat phase 008 as wholly unrepaired; some continuity/key-file repairs exist, but top-level and registry state remain inconsistent.
Treat implementation-summary.md presence as proof of completion; sampled summaries can still contain template text.
Treat old-path GLM reports as current state; they are historical but need tombstoning.
Blame fanout-run alone for threshold omission; buildLoopPrompt can emit the threshold when YAML passes it.
Use blocked test attempts as green verification evidence.
Assume the single decision-record.md covers all ADR phases.
Rely on evidence-marker lint for durable YAML comment hygiene.
